heading: Consistency with international obligations of United States
label: 2278 Consistency with international obligations of United States

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Prior to the promulgation of, or amendment to, any order or plan under a research and promotion program relating to research and promotion of any agricultural commodity or product, after , where such order or plan would provide for an assessment on imports, the Secretary of Agriculture shall consult with the United States Trade Representative regarding the consistency of the provisions of the order or plan with the international obligations of the United States.November 28, 1990

children.heading: Compliance with U.S. international obligations
children.label: b Compliance with U.S. international obligations
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall take all steps necessary and appropriate to ensure that any order or plan or amendment to such order or plan, and the implementation and enforcement of any order or plan or amendment to such order or plan, or program as it relates to imports is nondiscriminatory and in compliance with the international obligations of the United States, as interpreted by the United States Trade Representative.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: c Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as providing for a cause of action under this section.
